ui_policy.rule: Only the illustrated tutorial hand-drawn Whiteboard style is currently available. Other style entries may stay visible in the UI, but they must be disabled/greyed out and must not be selectable.

simple_trace.rule: Draw simple tutorial diagrams stroke by stroke with the visible marker hand. Use concrete objects, process arrows, comparison states, PPE items, checklists beside a larger visual anchor, and teacher-board labels. Do not use finished reference images for these scenes.

direct_reference.rule: Show complex graphics directly as a finished hand-drawn reference image in the same whiteboard marker style, then add live teacher annotations in the renderer.

mode_policy.mixed_video_rule: When a topic contains a complex physical scene or dense reference subject, include at least one direct_reference scene and at least one trace scene. Pure math or pure abstract concept videos may stay trace-only. This rule belongs only to the active illustrated tutorial hand-drawn style.

style_tokens.canvas: warm off-white classroom whiteboard, clean and roomy
style_tokens.title: blue handwritten title
style_tokens.risk: red risk marks and warning labels
style_tokens.emphasis: sunny yellow wavy underlines or small highlight blobs
style_tokens.hand: visible marker hand, pen tip touching the active stroke
style_tokens.subject_art: same marker/crayon texture for direct reference art and traced diagrams
style_tokens.teaching_layout: one large visual anchor first, then labels and annotations in the rhythm of the narration
style_tokens.lesson_feel: a teacher explaining at the board with diagrams, examples, risk marks, checks, and short takeaways

rich.rule: Every scene needs one clear visual anchor with 4-7 meaningful subject elements and 3-5 short labels. Rich means teacher-board substance, not a dense poster.

annotation_templates.use: Name a visible subject part from the margin.
annotation_templates.renderer_rule: Write a short Chinese label near the subject edge; optional small underline.

annotation_templates.use: Point from a short label to a named part.
annotation_templates.renderer_rule: Arrow must start or end near a readable label and must not sweep across the whole board.

annotation_templates.use: Emphasize a title or key term already written on the board.
annotation_templates.renderer_rule: Underline only readable text or a named concept.

annotation_templates.use: Mark a boundary, hazard edge, or equipment edge on direct reference art.
annotation_templates.renderer_rule: Tick must sit beside a label or beat target.

annotation_templates.use: Show a hazard near a risk label.
annotation_templates.renderer_rule: Small red rays or warning marks only next to a risk label.

annotation_templates.use: Confirm a correct PPE item or completed step.
annotation_templates.renderer_rule: Green check must sit beside the checked item label.

annotation_templates.use: Reject a wrong route, unsafe action, or failed communication.
annotation_templates.renderer_rule: Crossout must include or sit beside the reason label.

annotation_templates.use: Trace a safe route or process route.
annotation_templates.renderer_rule: Route line must connect named start/end labels.

annotation_templates.use: Explain a local detail only when the label names the zoomed detail.
annotation_templates.renderer_rule: Zoom box/circle is allowed only with a readable label and connector.

baked_image_policy.image_model_role: Generate clean subject artwork only. The renderer adds all readable handwriting and teacher annotations.
baked_image_policy.prompt_rule: Keep generated images text-free with open whitespace. Any box, circle, arrow, bracket, tick, underline, or badge baked into the image must be a real physical or symbolic part of the subject, never a teacher annotation placeholder.

scene_order_policy.rule: Opening scenes appear first; summary/review scenes appear last unless the user explicitly requests another order.
